summary: Proposal for a practical red gate around the headless improvement loop: separate scoreboard candidates from diagnostics, no-judge probes, measurement corrections, and targeted reruns; require blind-judge/safety checks before promotion; and fail promotion on source-scope bleed, fabricated unavailable facts, overfit evidence, or incomparable scoring.

models.review_method: manual review of saved local artifacts and scripts only

red_gate_purpose: Prevent diagnostic or targeted rows from being promoted as global performance evidence.
Prevent judge-leniency, source-scope bleed, and fabricated unavailable facts from hiding behind high EDD.
Keep useful local probes in the loop log without letting them enter the leaderboard or final claim path.

promotion_states.meaning: Local retrieval/source/code/judge-prep check, no judge run, no answer rerun, synthetic connection check, or incomplete judged metrics.
A rerun on a known failure case, small slice, or single question. It can explain a fix but cannot establish general performance.
Same answers rescored after evaluator or taxonomy correction. It may correct the historical record but is not a new RAG run.
Fresh run on a declared suite with complete retrieval metrics, complete judge quality metrics for non-abstention answers, abstention metrics, latency, and no red-gate failures.
A scoreboard candidate that beats or ties the current baseline under comparable settings and passes blind/safety/overfit checks.
A promotion candidate approved after red gate review, with final report language limited to the exact validated suite.
A row or proposal with a confirmed quality, safety, comparability, or overfit failure.

red_gate_checks.pass_condition: groundedness_avg and relevance_avg are present, parseable, and based on judged non-abstention answers; abstention cases are evaluated by abstention accuracy rather than fake groundedness/relevance.
red_gate_checks.fail_condition: Missing judge columns, no-judge mode, judge parse failures that leave quality blank, or EDD-like values computed from missing quality metrics.

red_gate_checks.pass_condition: Run records the question file/suite, case count, answer model, judge model, and whether the split is tune, holdout, untouched validation, or targeted.
red_gate_checks.fail_condition: Unknown case source, mixed targeted rerun plus full suite without labels, reused holdout treated as untouched, or silent suite slicing.

red_gate_checks.pass_condition: First validation score is preserved separately from same-answer rescoring and targeted retries.
red_gate_checks.fail_condition: A measurement correction, retry, or one-case targeted probe is presented as a fresh global score.

red_gate_checks.pass_condition: Before relying on near-ceiling EDD, run the existing blind calibration pack or equivalent: expected pass cases pass, planted bad answers fail, and failures are recorded.
red_gate_checks.fail_condition: Judge passes planted fabricated vendor/contact data, wrong-document bleed, wrong denial of PG evidence, or same-issuer scope mix.

red_gate_checks.pass_condition: Representative risky answers show correct issuer/project scope and no unsupported cross-document bleed, especially compare questions and short-alias municipality questions.
red_gate_checks.fail_condition: Wrong issuer, wrong project, same-issuer but wrong-document mix, or false 'not found' caused by too-narrow retrieval when evidence exists in the correct scope.

red_gate_checks.pass_condition: For final vendor, final contract amount, evaluation scores, private contact, and similar unavailable fields, the answer abstains while preserving verified public RFP facts.
red_gate_checks.fail_condition: Fabricates vendor/amount/contact data, converts missing final procurement data into a confident answer, or over-refuses verified public facts.

red_gate_checks.pass_condition: High EDD rows have a small representative answer review for directness, usefulness, citation clarity, and conciseness; known high-score-but-awkward answers are called out in report language.
red_gate_checks.fail_condition: Claims final answer-quality improvement from EDD alone when saved answers remain verbose, hard to cite, or report-unready.

red_gate_checks.pass_condition: Improvement is shown on a declared non-targeted validation set or clearly labeled as tune-only; targeted fixes are followed by a broader judged rerun before global claims.
red_gate_checks.fail_condition: Same failed holdout is repeatedly tuned against and then reported as strict held-out evidence, or one known case is used to justify a default change.

red_gate_checks.pass_condition: Latency improvement is accepted only if groundedness, relevance, abstention, and safety do not regress beyond declared tolerance.
red_gate_checks.fail_condition: Lower top_k or faster path is promoted despite quality regression, missed PG/detail evidence, or new source-scope risk.

what_fails_promotion: Any row with missing groundedness_avg or relevance_avg.
Any no-judge row, even if retrieval coverage, MRR, abstention, or latency look strong.
Any local diagnostic, code guard, calibration preparation, qualitative review, or prompt candidate with no scored answer/judge run.
Any measurement correction of the same answers when presented as a new run.
Any targeted rerun on known failures when presented as global validation.
Any run whose score depends on a judge that fails blind calibration bad cases.
Any answer that fabricates unavailable procurement/private-contact facts.
Any answer with confirmed wrong-document or wrong-issuer source-scope bleed.
Any faster configuration that loses material evidence quality, such as the observed top5 PG-detail miss compared with top8 on qv3_010.
Any claimed strict holdout/generalization score from a split already used for failure analysis and targeted repair unless it is explicitly labeled as remeasurement.

row_labeling_rules.report_rule: May be cited as diagnostic evidence or implementation preparation only.
May be cited for retrieval/answer behavior; must not be ranked or graphed as performance.
May explain a fix; requires broader judged rerun before promotion.
Use to correct historical interpretation; do not count as new performance.
Can enter leaderboard; still must pass red-gate checks before promoted claims.
Implementation smoke only; no answer-quality or RAG performance claims.

practical_application_to_current_loop: Treat L0 as first-run evidence but not automatically promoted because later notes found evaluator issues.
Treat L1 as corrected measurement of L0 answers, not a new run.
Treat L2/L3 as rejected global default evidence because speed improved but quality dropped.
Treat L4-L8 as diagnostics/calibration prep only.
Treat L9/L10 as targeted no-judge probes explaining why top8 is safer for qv3_010; do not score them.
Treat L11-L13 as candidate preparation and qualitative evidence, not performance improvements.
Before any next promoted default, require a judged non-targeted rerun plus blind calibration and representative red review.
needs_orchestrator_review: Whether near-ceiling EDD must always run blind calibration first, or only when promoted over the current best.
Exact tolerances for latency improvements: suggested default is no drop in groundedness/relevance over 0.10 average and no new safety red failures.
Whether corrected-measurement rows should appear in summary.md as a separate 'Corrected Measurement' table rather than the current diagnostic-only table.

commands_run: Manual proposal only; no external API/model calls
observed_results: loop_points.csv has L0-L13 with raw, corrected, local diagnostic, no-judge targeted probe, code guard, qualitative review, and prompt candidate labels.

aggregate_parallel_eval.py already excludes missing quality rows from rankings using diagnostic_only, but does not yet distinguish targeted rerun, measurement correction, calibration, or promotion states.
evaluate.py computes retrieval, judge quality, abstention, empty answer, and latency metrics; no-judge outputs can leave quality incomplete.
proposal output confined to worker_outputs/red_gate only.
